steps.title: Capture transport and access
steps.content: The poem compares books and poetry to vehicles that carry readers to distant lands. It also says even the poorest can take this journey without a toll, emphasizing that literature is accessible regardless of wealth.

steps.title: Reject unsupported readings
steps.content: A invents cultural connections, B turns accessibility into a claim about defying money, and D claims reading teaches more than travel. The poem's main idea is metaphorical transport through literature available to all means. The answer is C.
